methods.description: Procedure for preparing and analyzing pollen grains found in the oesophagal diverticulum of adult specimens, including sample handling, slide preparation, microscopic observation, identification, and quantification.

steps.description: Specimens collected were killed at -80°C and kept in a freezer.
We extracted the oesophagal diverticulum of adults, which is the part of the gut where the pollen is stored and not yet digested (Fig. 1).
steps.searchable_summary: Extract oesophagal diverticulum from adult specimens

steps.description: The diverticulum was opened on a microscope slide.
steps.searchable_summary: Open oesophagal diverticulum on a microscope slide

steps.description: After lipid extraction with diethyl ether, the pollen grains from the diverticulum were placed on a slide with glycerin jelly (Kaiser fourn, Merck Eurolab) containing basic fuchsin as a stain.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform lipid extraction on diverticulum contents and prepare slide with glycerin jelly and basic fuchsin stain
outputs: Prepared microscope slide with stained pollen grains

steps.description: Pollen grains were observed with an optic microscope ×400 (Olympus BH-2 with objective SPlan40, ×40).
steps.searchable_summary: Observe pollen grains using an optic microscope at 400x magnification

steps.description: The pollen grains were determined to family or species level at ×400.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify pollen grains to family or species level based on microscopic observation

steps.description: To quantify the amount of pollen, the slides were observed at ×20 and the total number of pollen grains in the diverticulum was estimated, taking in account its size, which ranges from 2 mm (empty crop) to 3 mm (full crop).
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify total pollen grains by observing slides at 20x magnification and estimating based on diverticulum size
outputs: Estimated total number of pollen grains
